workflow.name: Generate AI case studies from Feedspace testimonials with Google Gemini and publish to WordPress
workflow.description:
**Who is this for?**
This template is for teams who collect customer testimonials on feedpsace (via forms) and want to automatically convert them into professional case studies using AI and publish them to WordPress.

**What this workflow does**

This workflow listens for incoming testimonial data via a webhook, extracts the relevant fields, uses an AI agent to generate a complete case study (including title, sections, and structure), and publishes the final content directly to WordPress.

The AI is instructed to vary tone, angle, and structure across case studies to avoid repetitive content and improve SEO value.

**Requirements:**
- Feedspace account with webhook integration enabled
- Access to a WordPress site with REST API enabled
- An AI API key (Google Gemini or compatible model)

**Setup steps**
- Connect to Feedspace
	- Activate the workflow and copy the Production webhook URL
	- Go to Feedspace → Automations → Webhooks
	- Paste the webhook URL and activate it
	- See https://www.feedspace.io/help/automation/ for more information
- Add your AI API credentials to the AI model node
- Connect your WordPress account in the WordPress node
- Send testimonial data to the webhook in this format:
	- Reviewer name
	- Rating
	- Text feedback
	- Event or feedback type
	- Activate the workflow



**How it works**

1. Receives testimonial data through feedpsace webhook
2. Extracts reviewer name, rating, feedback, and event type
3. Filters for text-based testimonials
4. Uses an AI agent to:
5. Choose a unique case study angle and tone
6. Generate structured HTML content
7. Create an SEO-optimized title
8. Parses and validates the AI output
9. Publishes the generated case study to WordPress as a post

workflow.name: Generate AI-Powered Case Studies from Testimonials and Publish to WordPress

nodes.name: AI Agent - Generate Case Study & Title

parameters.text:
=I'm sending you a new customer testimonial to convert into a case study. TESTIMONIAL DATA: Customer: {{ $('Extract Testimonial Data').item.json.review_user }} Rating: {{ $('Extract Testimonial Data').item.json.rating }} stars Review: {{ $('Extract Testimonial Data').item.json.review }} INSTRUCTIONS: Step 1: MEMORY CHECK Look back at our conversation history. Have we created case studies before? If yes: - What angle did the previous case study use? - What tone and title formula? - Which features were highlighted? - Note this in your 'reasoning' field Step 2: CHOOSE VARIETY Based on what you remember: - Select a DIFFERENT angle from last time - Use a DIFFERENT tone - Apply a DIFFERENT title formula - Highlight DIFFERENT product features Step 3: CREATE CASE STUDY Generate a professional case study with:

Executive Summary

(or vary the header: The Breakthrough, The Success Story, etc.) 2-3 sentences summarizing the transformation

The Challenge

(or: The Problem, The Pain Point, etc.) Customer's problem before using the product

The Solution

(or: The Answer, How FeedSpace Helped, etc.) How the product solved their problem - highlight different features than recent case studies

The Results

(or: The Impact, The Outcome, etc.) Specific outcomes and metrics - vary between quantitative and qualitative focus

Customer Testimonial

{{ $('Extract Testimonial Data').item.json.review }}

Key Takeaways

(or: Key Benefits, Success Factors, etc.)
  • 3-4 bullet points highlighting different aspects than previous case studies
Step 4: GENERATE SEO TITLE Create title (50-60 characters) using one of the 5 formulas - rotate from previous usage Step 5: EXPLAIN YOUR CHOICES In the 'meta.reasoning' field, briefly explain: - If this is your first case study: "First case study, choosing [angle] approach" - If you have memory: "Previous case study used [X], so choosing [Y] for variety" CRITICAL: Return ONLY the JSON object. No markdown blocks, no backticks, no extra text.

options.systemMessage:
You are a professional marketing content writer who creates diverse, engaging case studies from customer testimonials. CRITICAL: You have conversation memory enabled. Before creating each case study, you should: 1. Mentally review what case studies you've created in our previous conversation turns 2. Notice patterns: What angles did I use? What tone? What title formulas? 3. Deliberately choose DIFFERENT approaches to create variety ANGLE OPTIONS (rotate through these): - Transformation Story: Focus on before/after journey - Problem-Solution: Emphasize pain points and resolution - ROI-Focused: Highlight metrics, upgrades, business impact - Feature Spotlight: Deep dive into specific capabilities - Integration Success: Emphasize connections and workflow - Efficiency Gains: Focus on time-saving and automation TONE OPTIONS (vary these): - Data-driven: Metrics and statistics focused - Emotional: Customer satisfaction and relief focused - Technical: Features and capabilities focused - Narrative: Story-driven with journey arc TITLE FORMULAS (rotate these): 1. "[Customer]: Achieves [Benefit] with [Product]" 2. "How [Customer] Increased [Metric] by [X]%" 3. "From [Problem] to [Solution]: [Customer] Story" 4. "[Customer] Leverages [Feature] to [Outcome]" 5. "How [Customer] Solved [Problem]" VARIATION STRATEGY: - If you just created a data-driven, ROI-focused case study with formula #2, now try emotional, transformation story with formula #1 - If you highlighted 'Google integration' last time, focus on 'widget customization' this time - If you used

Executive Summary last time, try

The Breakthrough this time - Keep content fresh by changing section headers, emphasized features, and narrative angles RESPONSE FORMAT: Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown, no code blocks): { "title": "SEO-optimized title (50-60 chars)", "content": "

Section

Content...

", "meta": { "angle": "transformation_story", "tone": "emotional", "title_formula": "formula_3", "features_highlighted": ["feature1", "feature2"], "reasoning": "Chose X because previous case study used Y" } } HTML RULES: -

for headers -

for paragraphs -

for quotes -

  • for bullets - for emphasis - NO markdown Your response must start with { and end with }

## Webhook & Notification
Receives Feedspace webhook, extracts data and checks if text feedback
## Generate case study with AI Agent

## Create Wordpress Post
Prepare the wordpress data and creates a post on WP.

## Generate AI Case Studies from Feedspace Testimonials
**Who is this for?**
Teams using Feedspace who want to automatically turn customer testimonials into SEO-optimized case studies and publish them on WordPress.

**Setup steps**
- Connect to Feedspace
	- Activate the workflow and copy the Production webhook URL
	- Go to Feedspace → Automations → Webhooks
	- Paste the webhook URL and activate it
	- See https://www.feedspace.io/help/automation/ for more information
- Add your AI API credentials to the AI model node
- Connect your WordPress account in the WordPress node
- Send testimonial data to the webhook in this format:
	- Reviewer name
	- Rating
	- Text feedback
	- Event or feedback type
	- Activate the workflow


**How it works**

1. Receives testimonial data through feedpsace webhook
2. Extracts reviewer name, rating, feedback, and event type
3. Filters for text-based testimonials
4. Uses an AI agent to:
5. Choose a unique case study angle and tone
6. Generate structured HTML content
7. Create an SEO-optimized title
8. Parses and validates the AI output
9. Publishes the generated case study to WordPress as a post

user.name: Feedspace
user.bio: Automate your testimonial workflow — pull in reviews from multiple sources, organize and tag feedback, and push curated content into websites, CMS platforms, or marketing channels.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
